all_issues.description: Small font size in the executive briefing body text (appears to be ~13-14px) reduces readability for extended reading; consider increasing to 15-16px minimum
Right sidebar content appears cramped with tight vertical spacing; 'BILLS BY LEGISLATIVE STAGE' section rows could benefit from increased line-height for clarity
Color coding in 'REGULATORY STATUS' table uses orange, green, and blue squares without visible legend; users may not immediately understand what each color represents
Italicized 'Fun fact' text in the blue box at bottom of briefing uses light gray on white background, creating lower contrast than body text above it
State abbreviation buttons at bottom ('CA', 'CT', etc.) have minimal padding and could be difficult to click accurately on touch devices if this dashboard is responsive

Body text in 'OPENING' and 'KEY DEVELOPMENTS' sections is too small and dense. The paragraph text appears to be around 12-14px on a mobile viewport, making it difficult to read comfortably. Line-height also appears tight, reducing scanability.

Text overflow/truncation in date header: 'Tuesday, March 24, 2026 / Mar 21 – 27' is cut off with 'Sc' visible on right edge, indicating content extends beyond viewport or improper handling of the date range display.

Tab navigation (DIRECTOR REVIEW, TIMELINE, PFAS MAP, ARC...) is cramped and the last tab appears truncated. On 375px width, these tabs should either stack or have better horizontal scrolling indicators.
Long continuous paragraphs without visual breaks or bullet points in 'KEY DEVELOPMENTS' reduce readability on mobile. Content needs better segmentation or truncation with 'read more' functionality.
Inconsistent heading hierarchy and styling—'OPENING' and 'KEY DEVELOPMENTS' use uppercase labels but lack visual distinction from body content. Section spacing could be more consistent.
Sparse content — only one briefing entry visible for the entire 2026 section; appears to be incomplete archive listing with significant empty space below
Excessive whitespace — large empty area below the single briefing entry creates poor visual balance and suggests missing content or broken pagination
Date format inconsistency — subtitle uses 'WEEKLY BRIEFINGS' (small caps) but intro text uses lowercase 'compliance briefings'; minor inconsistency in styling conventions
Large empty white space below the footer (approximately 40% of viewport) suggests missing content, broken layout, or incomplete page load
Footer text 'COMPLIANCE INTELLIGENCE — MARCH 22, 2026' uses different styling/weight than header; inconsistent typographic hierarchy
Date range text '2026-03-14 — 2026-03-20' uses monospace font which is visually inconsistent with body text; minor but noticeable

Abbreviation acronyms (PFAS, PFOA, PFOS, PTFE, CAA, CERCLA, etc.) use teal/cyan color (#008B8B-like) which has insufficient contrast against white background for extended readability; recommend darker color or bold weight for clarity.
Description text in definition rows (right column) appears to have variable line-height and spacing, creating inconsistent visual rhythm across entries.
Section headers (CHEMICALS, US FEDERAL) use different font weights and spacing; could be standardized for stronger visual hierarchy.
Abbreviation tags (PFAS, PFOA, PPOS, PTFE) are small and could benefit from slightly larger font size for better mobile readability
Description text for chemical entries is left-aligned and relatively narrow, creating short line lengths that could be optimized for better scannability
Tag styling uses inconsistent sizing — some tags appear more cramped than others due to varying text lengths without apparent padding normalization
Text truncation in 'FTC Green Guides Update: Recyclability & Compostabil...' — title is cut off with ellipsis, reducing clarity of the full deadline item
Inconsistent spacing between jurisdiction sections (Federal has dense item list, Alabama/Alaska/Arizona sections below show 'No tracked deadlines' with excessive whitespace)
Gray placeholder text 'No tracked deadlines' for empty state sections has low contrast and unclear visual hierarchy compared to active deadline items
Jurisdiction label 'Federal' appears disconnected from its content due to large vertical gap; consider tighter visual grouping

Multiple deadline entries have truncated text with '...' ellipsis, making titles unreadable (e.g., 'EPA TSCA CBI Claims Ir...', 'EPA TSCA Chemical Da...', 'FTC Green Guides Upda...'). Full titles are essential for compliance information.

Right column content is cut off/overflowing on mobile (375px width). Tag badges like 'TSCA', 'EPR', 'PFAS' and associated content ('L', 'M', 'H' urgency indicators) are partially hidden or misaligned.
Urgency legend at bottom uses very small text ('High (<6 mo)', 'Medium (6-12 mo)', 'Low (>12 mo)') with minimal contrast. Font size appears below recommended 14px minimum for mobile.
Inconsistent text truncation lengths across deadline items creates visual instability and suggests responsive design breakpoints not optimized for 375px width.
Tab navigation at top ('MAPS', 'PFAS', 'EPR', etc.) may have overflow or text wrapping issues on narrow viewport; readability of full tab names unclear.

Text truncation in deadline descriptions: 'Manufacturer registration required under DTSC (AB 34...' and 'Ban on PFAS in all cleaning products, textiles, food...' and 'PFAS labeling required across covered product catego...' are cut off with ellipsis, making full content unreadable without interaction.
Low contrast on inactive jurisdiction text (Alabama, Alaska, Arizona, Arkansas, Delaware, Florida): light gray text on white background makes 'No tracked deadlines' very difficult to read.
Inconsistent spacing/alignment: State abbreviations (CA, CO, CT) are not vertically centered relative to their jurisdiction names, creating visual misalignment.

Inconsistent badge styling: 'PFAS' badges appear in different colors (red, blue) without clear visual distinction in legend—severity indicators (MEDIUM, LOW, HIGH) use different styling than the topic badges.
Text truncation on state abbreviations (AL, AK, AZ, AR, CA, CO, CT) and jurisdiction codes — text is cut off and unreadable
Deadline text for Alabama, Alaska, Arizona, and Arkansas states shows 'No tracked deadline' in very low contrast gray text, difficult to read against light background
Card titles in right column (EPA TRI PFAS Reporting, Manufacturer registration, DTSC enforcement, Ban on PFAS, PFAS labeling required) are truncated with ellipsis — full text not visible
Multiple empty/incomplete card display — right column shows partial regulatory items that appear cut off or incomplete
Inconsistent spacing and alignment between left jurisdiction list and right content column — visual hierarchy unclear on mobile
Mixed visual treatment of deadline urgency legend at bottom (colored boxes with Medium/High/Low labels) appears disconnected from main content flow

Multiple regulation titles are truncated with ellipsis ('...'), making full content inaccessible without hover or expansion. Examples: 'FTC Green Guides Update: Recyclability & Compostabil...', 'PPWR: EU Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation Ta...', 'Maine EPR: Municipal Cost Reimbursement Program Expa...'

Timeline header shows quarter markers (Q3 2026, Q4 2026) but the horizontal timeline grid is not clearly aligned with row content, making it difficult to visually correlate deadlines to quarters at a glance
The red vertical bar indicator on 'Maine EPR: Phase 2 Stewardship Fee Increases' appears incomplete or cut off on the right edge, inconsistent with other visual treatments in the timeline
Jurisdiction labels (Federal, CA, CO, EU, ME, MD) use small text with minimal visual hierarchy compared to regulation titles, making quick scanning less efficient

Multiple text truncations in the main content area: 'FTC Green Guides Upda', 'SB 54: Producer Registr', 'SB 54: 30% Single-Use', 'SB 54: 65% Single-Use', 'SB 54: 100% Recyclable', 'HB 22-1355: Producer R', 'HB 22-1355: Phase I Rec', 'PPWR: EU Packaging ar', 'PPWR: Reusable Packa', 'Maine EPR: Phase 2 Ste' — text cuts off mid-word making titles difficult to read
Right sidebar content appears cramped with small badge labels ('EPR', 'RE', 'LO') that lack clear spacing and visual hierarchy; badges are difficult to scan at this mobile width
Small font size on jurisdiction labels ('CA', 'CO') and urgency legend at bottom ('High (<6 mo)', 'Medium (6-12 mo)', 'Low (>12 mo)') creates readability strain on mobile screens
Horizontal scrolling likely needed for the full table header (MAPS, PFAS, EPR, REACH, TIMELINES, PFAS, E...) — the 'E' column appears cut off, indicating overflow
Date formatting is inconsistent across entries: some use 'Dec 31, 2026', others 'Jan 01, 2027', 'Jan 01, 2028' — minor but inconsistent presentation

issues.description: Metric cards (5/10, 3/10, 5/10) use large orange and red numbers that create high contrast but may be difficult to scan quickly; consider adding visual hierarchy or icons to differentiate metrics at a glance
Section headings use inconsistent styling: 'What's Working' is teal, 'Questions Raised But Not Answered' is red/brown; consider standardizing heading colors across the dashboard
The italicized quote/insight block uses a left border but lacks visual distinction from body text; font size and padding could be more prominent for emphasis
Metadata line 'Generated 2026-03-24...' uses small gray text that is legible but could be visually distinguished better from main content (e.g., placed in a subtle footer or card)

desktop.notes: Dashboard is well-structured with clear hierarchy and readable text; no critical accessibility or layout failures detected, only minor consistency refinements suggested.

issues.description: Command text 'python run.py --director-review to regenerate' uses monospace font that is quite small and may be difficult to read on mobile; consider larger font size or code block styling
The quoted section ('Promising skeleton with real data...') uses italic text which reduces readability on small screens; the left border accent is good but text contrast could be improved
Three metric cards (5/10, 3/10, 5/10) appear to stack vertically on 375px mobile — verify they fit within viewport width without horizontal scroll
Bullet point list items have varying line lengths; some wrap awkwardly. Consider consistent left padding or line-length optimization for mobile

mobile.notes: Dashboard is functional and mostly readable on mobile; minor readability improvements needed for code snippets and italicized quote text.

issues.description: Legend text is small and difficult to read, especially the descriptive text in parentheses (e.g., 'green = high, amber = medium, gray = low'). Consider larger font size or improved contrast.
The right panel shows placeholder text 'Select a state to see details' with excessive empty space. This creates visual imbalance and wastes screen real estate on a 1440px desktop.
Legend is duplicated at top (inline with counts) and bottom (below map). Consider consolidating to avoid redundancy and improve clarity.
Corner dots on state tiles indicating 'evidence confidence' are very small and could be missed by users. Consider tooltip or larger indicator.
State tiles have inconsistent spacing and alignment, creating a grid-like pattern that feels slightly scattered rather than precisely organized.

desktop.notes: Dashboard is functional but has minor readability and layout inefficiencies; placeholder UI and small legend text should be addressed.

issues.description: Brown banner text is white on dark brown background with adequate contrast, but the disclaimer is dense and small—consider breaking into bullet points or increasing line-height for mobile readability.
Legend items (Early Signals, Rulemaking, Bill Introduced, etc.) are presented in a multi-column grid that may wrap awkwardly on very small phones—consider single-column stacking for consistency.

Color-coded legend uses varied color blocks (gray, red, blue, purple, orange) but no actual map/visualization is visible in this screenshot—verify the map component renders correctly below the legend on mobile.

mobile.notes: Dashboard is functionally sound with good contrast and hierarchy; minor layout optimization recommended for mobile legend wrapping and disclaimer readability.

issues.description: State abbreviations on the map are very small (2-letter codes like 'WA', 'MT', 'ND') and may be difficult to read at normal viewing distance, especially for users with vision impairments.
Tab navigation uses inconsistent styling — 'EPR' tab is highlighted in orange/gold while other inactive tabs use gray text, creating visual confusion about active state clarity.
Right-side panel (CA details) has dense text blocks under 'ACTIVE & PROPOSED PROGRAMS' and 'KEY DATES' with minimal spacing between items, reducing scannability.

desktop.notes: Dashboard is functional with good information hierarchy; minor readability concerns with small map labels and dense text blocks in side panel do not significantly impair usability.

mobile.notes: Modal content is cut off requiring scroll, and header text contrast needs verification against WCAG standards.

Legend at bottom left uses italicized fine print text that is difficult to scan quickly; considers using non-italic or bold for category labels.

desktop.notes: Dashboard is well-organized with good contrast and clear hierarchy; minor readability issue in footer text that should be addressed for compliance documentation purposes.

Badge labels ('PRIORITY', 'Primary') use different styling and capitalization conventions, reducing visual consistency.

issues.description: Italicized body text in the summary paragraph is difficult to read at length; the italic style reduces scannability and may strain readers. Consider using regular font weight for better readability.
Line length appears excessive (likely 80+ characters), reducing reading comfort. Standard best practice is 50-75 characters per line for body text.
Bullet point text is not constrained to a narrower measure and runs full-width, creating long lines that reduce readability compared to the narrower summary section above.

desktop.notes: Dashboard is clean and well-organized with good visual hierarchy and spacing; minor readability improvements recommended for italicized text and line length.

issues.description: Body text in the summary section uses italic styling, which reduces readability on mobile screens. The contrast and font weight combination makes sustained reading more difficult.
The bullet point text under 'KEY DEVELOPMENTS' appears to be cut off at the bottom of the viewport. Content may be truncated or require scrolling to view completely.
Inconsistent spacing between section headers (all-caps labels like 'END OF WEEK SUMMARY' vs 'KEY DEVELOPMENTS') and their content could be more uniform for visual hierarchy.

mobile.notes: Dashboard is functional and readable overall; minor readability concerns with italicized body text and potential content cutoff at bottom are the only notable issues.

issues.description: Italic text in watch list items (action items and analysis) has reduced contrast against white background and is harder to scan quickly. Consider using regular weight with different visual treatment (e.g., background highlight, left border).
Watch list items use a thin left red border that may not be sufficiently visible as a visual divider. The border-to-text spacing could be increased for better visual hierarchy.
State abbreviations (VA, MD, NY, MN, NJ, WA) are inconsistently styled — some are bold, some are not. Standardize formatting for visual consistency.
Line length of body text appears to approach or exceed 90 characters per line in some sections, which can reduce readability. Consider narrower max-width for paragraph text.

desktop.notes: Dashboard is well-structured with clear hierarchy; minor readability improvements needed for italic text and line length consistency.

issues.description: Body text line length exceeds optimal mobile readability. Long paragraphs under 'PFAS' section create dense text blocks that are difficult to scan on 375px viewport.
Watch List items have inconsistent visual hierarchy. Mixed use of italics, regular text, and parenthetical information makes it unclear what is action-required vs. supporting detail.
Metadata text ('Generated 2026-03-23...') uses lighter gray that may have insufficient contrast against white background for WCAG AA compliance.
Left border accent on Watch List items creates visual emphasis, but truncated text below ('MD — 4 bills passed...') suggests content may be cut off at viewport edge.

mobile.notes: Dashboard is functionally readable but has moderate text density and contrast concerns for mobile accessibility; content structure could be more scannable.

issues.description: The search field placeholder text 'Search articl' appears truncated or cut off in the top navigation, reducing clarity of its purpose.
The 'hide sidebar' link in the Executive Briefing section uses a right arrow symbol (►) that may not be immediately clear to all users as an interactive element.
The metric cards in the Regulatory Status section use inconsistent icon sizes and alignments across different regulatory frameworks (PFAS, EPR, REACH, TSCA, PROP65).
The 'Bills by Legislative Stage' section has narrow column widths for stage names (e.g., 'ENACTED', 'ADVANCED'), which could cause wrapping on smaller viewport adjustments.

desktop.notes: Dashboard is professionally designed with good information hierarchy; minor truncation and spacing inconsistencies don't significantly impact usability on desktop.

issues.description: Body text in 'OPENING' and 'KEY DEVELOPMENTS' sections is small and dense with poor line spacing, making it difficult to read on mobile. Text appears to be around 14px or smaller with minimal leading.

Text truncation visible in date range ('Tuesday, March 24, 2026 / Mar 21 – 27' appears cut off with 'Sc' visible on right edge), indicating horizontal overflow or improper text wrapping on mobile viewport.

Tab navigation ('DIRECTOR REVIEW', 'TIMELINE', 'PFAS MAP', 'ARC...') appears cut off on the right ('ARC' truncated), suggesting horizontal overflow or insufficient space allocation for tab labels.

issues.description: Body text in 'KEY DEVELOPMENTS' and 'OUTLOOK' sections is small (appears ~13-14px) with moderate contrast against white background. While readable, could strain users during extended reading sessions.

Right sidebar content ('REGULATORY STATUS', 'PFAS LEGISLATIVE INTEL', 'BILLS BY LEGISLATIVE STAGE') has inconsistent spacing and padding; colored status indicators (squares) in the table lack clear labels or legend in immediate context.
Metric cards at top use different styling for urgency labels (e.g., '2 HIGH', '1 MED', '3 HIGH') with inconsistent capitalization and spacing; color coding is present but not uniformly applied.
The 'hide sidebar >' link in top right is subtle and low contrast; could be missed as an affordance for collapsing the navigation.

desktop.notes: Dashboard is functionally sound with good information hierarchy; minor readability and consistency refinements would improve polish.

issues.description: Body text in 'OPENING' and 'KEY DEVELOPMENTS' sections uses small font size with poor contrast against white background. Text appears to be 12-14px, making it difficult to read on mobile.

Text content in 'KEY DEVELOPMENTS' section is severely truncated and cut off at the bottom of the visible area without indication of continuation. Multiple sentences end abruptly (e.g., 'under p', 'me', 'for f').
Long paragraph text blocks lack line-height spacing, creating dense walls of text that reduce readability on mobile. Paragraphs need better vertical breathing room.
Tab navigation ('DIRECTOR REVIEW', 'TIMELINE', 'PFAS MAP', 'ARCH...') appears to overflow or truncate on 375px viewport. The rightmost tab is cut off completely.
Section headers ('OPENING', 'KEY DEVELOPMENTS') use teal color while other UI elements use different accent colors, creating minor visual inconsistency.

mobile.notes: Critical text truncation and readability issues make this mobile dashboard unusable; content is cut off and difficult to parse.
